# Project Docs Skeleton

Use this as a starting point for docs in a new project repo or wiki.

## Suggested top-level docs

### README / Project Overview
Include:
- what the project is
- why it exists
- current status
- where to find spec and architecture docs

### Project Spec
Include:
- problem statement
- goals
- non-goals
- key user/system behaviors
- constraints
- assumptions

### Architecture Overview
Include:
- high-level system shape
- major components
- interfaces/data flow
- important tradeoffs
- unresolved design questions

### Development / Contribution Notes
Include:
- local setup
- test commands
- repo conventions
- PR expectations

### Operational or Runbook Notes
Include when relevant:
- deployment or environment notes
- secrets/config guidance
- troubleshooting basics

## Practical rule

Don’t try to create perfect docs at bootstrap.
Create just enough structure that the project has visible places for truth to live.
